title: spanish rice
instructions: Heat the avocado oil in a stainless steel sauté pan over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook, stirring as needed, until softened and starting to lightly brown.
Add the minced garlic and stir briefly until fragrant.
Add the jasmine rice and cook for a few minutes, stirring often. It is okay if the rice picks up a little color.
Add the tomato paste and stir to coat the rice. Add the cumin and stir for a few seconds to bloom the spice.
If there is browning on the bottom of the pan, add a splash of the vegetable broth to deglaze, scraping up the browned bits.
Transfer everything to a rice cooker. Add the remaining vegetable broth, using about 3 scant cups total.
Cook on the normal white rice setting.
Once complete, fluff the rice and serve.
notes: If you deglaze the sauté pan with broth, it can make transferring everything to the rice cooker a little messier, but it adds good flavor. Use about 3 scant cups broth total, including any broth used to deglaze. The mixed setting might help prevent overcooking if your rice cooker has it.
